children.heading: .—Authority
children.label: a .—Authority
children.text: The Governor of a State or the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, or the Virgin Islands, or the commanding general of the District of Columbia National Guard, as the case may be, with the consent of the Secretary concerned, may order a member of the National Guard to perform Active Guard and Reserve duty, as defined by , pursuant to .section 101(d)(6) of title 10section 502(f) of this title

children.heading: .—Duties
children.label: b .—Duties
children.text: A member of the National Guard performing duty under subsection (a) may perform the additional duties specified in to the extent that the performance of those duties does not interfere with the performance of the member’s primary Active Guard and Reserve duties of organizing, administering, recruiting, instructing, and training the reserve components.section 502(f)(2) of this title

Notwithstanding and subsection (b) of this section, the Governor of a State or the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico, Guam, or the Virgin Islands, or the commanding general of the District of Columbia National Guard, as the case may be, may, at the request of the Secretary concerned, order a member of the National Guard to perform Active Guard and Reserve duty for purposes of performing training of the regular components of the armed forces as the primary duty.section 101(d)(6)(A) of title 10
Training performed under paragraph (1) must be in compliance with the requirements of .section 502(f)(2)(B)(i) of this title
No more than 100 personnel may be granted a waiver by a Secretary concerned under paragraph (1) at a time.
The authority under paragraph (1) shall terminate on .October 1, 2024
